title: ophioglossum vulgatum var. luerssenii
intro: Ophioglossum vulgatum var. luerssenii is a variety of the adder's tongue fern, a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Ophioglossaceae family. Traditionally, this plant is used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. It is believed to have health benefits such as reducing swelling and promoting wound healing, as well as aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues like bronchitis and asthma. Ophioglossum vulgatum var. luerssenii can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions for medicinal use, although its safety and efficacy as a treatment for various ailments should be further researched and confirmed.
